ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth / DNSpionage / BondUpdater pattern
ucs.description: Hunts the high-entropy, long-subdomain DNS query bursts that APT34's signature implants (Helminth, ISMAgent, ALMA Communicator, DNSpionage, BondUpdater, Karkoff) use as their primary C2 channel. Every major OilRig campaign since 2016 has carried a DNS-tunnelling component, often as the *only* egress from segmented victim networks, which makes this one of the highest-fidelity behavioural pivots for this specific actor.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count from datamodel=Network_Resolution where Network_Resolution.record_type IN ("A","AAAA","TXT","CNAME") by _time span=10m Network_Resolution.src Network_Resolution.query Network_Resolution.record_type
| rename Network_Resolution.* AS *
| eval qlen=len(query), labels=split(query,"."), label_count=mvcount(labels), first_label=mvindex(labels,0), first_label_len=len(first_label)
| where label_count >= 3 AND first_label_len >= 30
| eval parent_domain=mvindex(labels,label_count-2).".".mvindex(labels,label_count-1)
| stats dc(query) AS unique_subdomains, avg(first_label_len) AS avg_first_label_len, max(first_label_len) AS max_first_label_len, values(record_type) AS rtypes, values(query) AS sample_queries, count AS query_count by src parent_domain
| where unique_subdomains >= 25 AND avg_first_label_len >= 30
| sort - unique_subdomains
| `security_content_ctime(_time)`
ucs.kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — DNS tunnelling C2 (Helminth / DNSpionage / BondUpdater)
let _browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe"]);
let _benign_resolvers = dynamic(["svchost.exe","searchindexer.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe","smartscreen.exe"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend QueryName = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(QueryName)
| extend Labels = split(QueryName, ".")
| extend LabelCount = array_length(Labels)
| where LabelCount >= 3                                  // skip TLD-only / 2-label
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(Labels[0])
| extend FirstLabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where FirstLabelLen >= 30                              // OilRig encoded subdomains run 30-63 chars
| extend ParentDomain = strcat(tostring(Labels[LabelCount-2]), ".", tostring(Labels[LabelCount-1]))
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_browsers)       // browsers do legit long DNS
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_benign_resolvers)
| where ParentDomain !endswith "in-addr.arpa"           // PTR lookups
| where ParentDomain !has_any ("akamai","cloudfront","azureedge","trafficmanager","office.com","microsoft.com","office365.com","windows.com","google.com","googleapis.com","gstatic.com","apple.com","icloud.com")
| summarize UniqueSubdomains = dcount(QueryName),
            AvgFirstLabelLen = avg(FirstLabelLen),
            MaxFirstLabelLen = max(FirstLabelLen),
            QueryCount = count(),
            Processes = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 8),
            ProcessCmds = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 8),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QueryName, 8)
            by DeviceName, DeviceId, ParentDomain, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where UniqueSubdomains >= 20 and AvgFirstLabelLen >= 30
| order by UniqueSubdomains desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — DNS tunnelling C2 via Windows DNS server logs
let _benign_parents = dynamic(["akamai","cloudfront","azureedge","trafficmanager","office.com","microsoft.com","office365.com","windows.com","google.com","googleapis.com","gstatic.com","apple.com","icloud.com"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where QueryType in ("A","AAAA","TXT","CNAME")
| extend QueryName = tolower(Name)
| where isnotempty(QueryName)
| extend Labels = split(QueryName, ".")
| extend LabelCount = array_length(Labels)
| where LabelCount >= 3
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(Labels[0])
| extend FirstLabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where FirstLabelLen >= 30
| extend ParentDomain = strcat(tostring(Labels[LabelCount-2]), ".", tostring(Labels[LabelCount-1]))
| where ParentDomain !endswith "in-addr.arpa"
| where ParentDomain !has_any (_benign_parents)
| summarize UniqueSubdomains = dcount(QueryName),
            AvgFirstLabelLen = avg(FirstLabelLen),
            MaxFirstLabelLen = max(FirstLabelLen),
            QueryCount = count(),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QueryName, 8)
            by ClientIP, Computer, ParentDomain, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where UniqueSubdomains >= 20 and AvgFirstLabelLen >= 30
| order by UniqueSubdomains desc
ucs.rationale: DNS-tunnel C2 is APT34's *signature* egress channel — Palo Alto Unit 42, Cisco Talos and FireEye/Mandiant have all documented Helminth, DNSpionage, ALMA Communicator, BondUpdater and Karkoff using base32/hex-encoded subdomains 30-63 chars long against attacker-registered second-level domains. The combined predicate (long first label + non-browser/non-system parent + bursty unique-subdomain volume

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) Office-macro → PowerShell/VBS stager → schtasks persistence chain (BondUpdater / Helminth initial access)
ucs.description: Hunts APT34's documented spearphishing chain where a weaponised .doc/.xls macro spawns cscript/wscript or powershell, which then registers a scheduled task for backdoor persistence within minutes. This exact 3-step sequence (Office → script-host/PowerShell → schtasks) is the hallmark Helminth, BondUpdater and QUADAGENT delivery pattern reported by FireEye, Cisco Talos, ClearSky and Microsoft (Hazel Sandstorm).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) AS stager_time values(Processes.process) AS stager_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) AS office_parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner dest user [
    | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` min(_time) AS schtask_time values(Processes.process) AS schtask_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" (Processes.process="*create*" OR Processes.process="*CREATE*" OR Processes.process="*/sc *") by Processes.dest Processes.user
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
  ]
| eval delta_sec = abs(schtask_time - stager_time)
| where delta_sec <= 600
| table dest user office_parent process_name stager_cmd schtask_cmd delta_sec
| `security_content_ctime(stager_time)`
ucs.kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — Office macro stager + scheduled-task persistence inside 10 minutes
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChainWindow = 10m;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let Stagers = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let MacroSpawn =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
    | where FileName in~ (Stagers)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project StagerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              OfficeCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              StagerImage = FileName,
              StagerCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              StagerSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/CREATE","-create","/sc ","/SC ")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project SchTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, SchCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SchParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, SchParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner MacroSpawn on DeviceId, AccountName
| where SchTime between (StagerTime .. StagerTime + ChainWindow)
| extend ChainDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SchTime, StagerTime)
| project StagerTime, SchTime, ChainDelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent, OfficeCmd,
          StagerImage, StagerCmd, StagerSHA256,
          SchParent, SchParentCmd, SchCmd
| order by StagerTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — Office macro stager + schtasks persistence (Sentinel ASIM, falls back to SecurityEvent 4688)
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChainWindow = 10m;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let Stagers = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let MacroSpawn =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (OfficeParents)
    | where TargetProcessName has_any (Stagers)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | extend StagerImageName = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | extend OfficeParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | project StagerTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              OfficeParentName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
              StagerImageName, TargetProcessCommandLine;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where TargetProcessName endswith "schtasks.exe"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/CREATE","-create","/sc ","/SC ")
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| project SchTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          SchCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          SchParent = ParentProcessName
| join kind=inner MacroSpawn on DvcHostname, ActorUsername
| where SchTime between (StagerTime .. StagerTime + ChainWindow)
| extend ChainDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SchTime, StagerTime)
| project StagerTime, SchTime, ChainDelaySec,
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          OfficeParentName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          StagerImageName, TargetProcessCommandLine,
          SchParent, SchCmd
| order by StagerTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 OilRig - Office Application Spawning Script Host or PowerShell Stager
id: 5b3b8e30-2f2c-4c4a-8b35-1e7a6f3c4d12
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Office (Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook) or Equation Editor spawning a scripting / LOLBin stager - the first hop of APT34 Helminth/BondUpdater/QUADAGENT delivery.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/dns-tunneling-in-the-wild-overview-of-oilrigs-dns-tunneling/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g0049
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise Office add-ins that shell out to script hosts
  - Internal macro-driven automation (scope to user populations that should never run macros)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Public technical write-ups on QUADAGENT (FireEye), BondUpdater (Palo Alto Unit 42), Helminth (Cisco Talos) and OopsIE (ClearSky) all describe the *same* three-step delivery — phished Office doc → cscript/wscript or powershell stager → schtasks-registered persistence task. Joining the macro-spawn event to a same-host, same-user schtasks /create within a 10-minute window encodes that exact chain and
